pullRequest.title: Fix: `uefi decode` returns success when no EFI firmware volumes are found
pullRequest.body:
`chipsec_util uefi decode` always exited with code `0` even when the input file contained no parseable EFI content (e.g. a non-firmware file), because `decode_uefi_region` returned `None` unconditionally and the command never propagated failure.

## Changes

- **`chipsec/library/uefi/spi.py` — `parse_uefi_region_from_file`**: Changed return type `None` → `List['EFI_MODULE']`, returning the parsed tree so callers can detect empty results.

- **`chipsec/library/uefi/spi.py` — `decode_uefi_region`**: Changed return type `None` → `bool`. Fixed NVRAM detection to use `if not fwtype` instead of `if fwtype is None`, since `identify_EFI_NVRAM` returns `''` (not `None`) when no format is found. Captures the return value of `parse_EFI_variables` and returns `bool(tree) or nvram_ok` so that a failure in both EFI volume parsing and NVRAM parsing correctly produces `False`.

- **`chipsec/utilcmd/uefi_cmd.py` — `UEFICommand.decode`**: Checks `decode_uefi_region` return value and sets `self.ExitCode = ExitCode.ERROR` on failure, producing a non-zero exit code.

- **`tests/utilcmd/uefi_cmd/test_uefi_cmd.py`** (new): Unit tests covering the failure paths of `uefi decode`:
  - Non-firmware file content → `ExitCode.ERROR`
  - Missing/non-existent file → `ExitCode.ERROR`

```bash
# Before
$ chipsec_util uefi decode ~/.bashrc -n
[CHIPSEC] Parsing EFI volumes from '/home/user/.bashrc'..
$ echo $?
0   # wrong

# After
$ chipsec_util uefi decode ~/.bashrc -n
[CHIPSEC] Parsing EFI volumes from '/home/user/.bashrc'..
[CHIPSEC][ERROR] Could not parse EFI firmware volumes from '/home/user/.bashrc'
$ echo $?
16  # ExitCode.ERROR
```

pullRequest.title: Fix objtool errors
pullRequest.body:
Fix kernel module compilation for kernels with stricter objtool checks.

- Fixes compilation on NixOS with linux-6.16.11 (and possibly other versions).
    -  Nix note: Some changes will also be needed in the chipsec nixpkgs package. It also does not solve the issue of chipsec not finding the kernel module. I will open a separate PR with some feature suggestions that will allow for more cleanly loading the module in setups with non-standard kernel module paths.
- Should resolve #2563 as well.


As noted in 24c48f4f8be8249f2b6d413b6e0d1c12bc7d90bf, it might make sense to use gas instead for the linux modules. A properly annotated .S file compiles "as-is" (setctx_cpuid remaining a function), and might prevent similar future issues, but not sure if this switch it is desired.
